channel.title: Srinjoy Bhuiya — Academic Portfolio on Srinjoy Bhuiya
channel.description: Recent content in Srinjoy Bhuiya — Academic Portfolio on Srinjoy Bhuiya

item.title: Getting Started with Hugo: Building an Academic Portfolio
item.description:
Building a personal academic website used to mean wrestling with WordPress plugins or paying for hosted services. With Hugo and Docker, you can have a fast, self-hosted, fully customisable site up and running in an afternoon.

This post walks through the approach I used for my own portfolio.

Why Hugo?

Hugo is a static site generator written in Go. It converts Markdown files and data files (JSON, YAML) into a complete HTML website. Key advantages for academics:

item.title: Streamflow Prediction in Snow-Dominated Basins: A Research Update
item.description:
My thesis work at the University of Alberta focuses on a challenging problem at the intersection of deep learning and hydrology: predicting river streamflow in snow-dominated basins where historical data is scarce.

The Problem

River flow prediction is critical for:

  • Water resource management — municipal supply, agriculture
  • Flood early warning — safety and infrastructure planning
  • Climate impact assessment — understanding changing snowmelt patterns

Traditional hydrological models rely on well-calibrated physical parameters that require years of measurements. In remote or recently instrumented basins, these measurements simply don’t exist.

item.title: About
item.description:
About Me

I am a Master’s student in Computing Science (Thesis) at the University of Alberta, working under the supervision of Dr. Nilanjan Ray at the VISUAL Laboratory.

My research sits at the intersection of deep learning, computer vision, and real-world AI applications — ranging from biomedical imaging to environmental monitoring and time-series forecasting.

Research Focus

My current thesis work focuses on developing large-scale pretrained models for river streamflow prediction in snow-dominated and data-scarce environments. I am particularly interested in:

item.title: Contact
item.description:
Let’s Connect

I am always happy to discuss research, collaborations, and opportunities.

Channel Details
Email srinjoybhuiya@gmail.com
LinkedIn linkedin.com/in/srinjoy-bhuiya
GitHub github.com/Srinjoycode
Location Edmonton, Canada

Research Collaboration

If you are interested in collaborating on research in:

  • Generative AI and LLMs
  • Computer vision and medical imaging
  • Environmental AI and Earth observation
  • Time-series forecasting

Please feel free to reach out via email with a brief description of your project.

item.title: Projects
item.description:
Featured Projects

Below are selected projects from my academic and professional experience. For a full list please see the Publications and Experience sections on the home page, or view my CV.

Streamflow Prediction with Pretrained Fusion Attention Models (In Progress)

Institution: University of Alberta
Supervisor: Dr. Nilanjan Ray

Developing long-term seasonal river water-flow forecasting models for data-scarce snow-dominated basins using large-scale pretraining on a novel attention-fusion architecture. Targeting the Bow River Basin near Calgary.
